handoff_meta.purpose: Continue work on the Change Management Platform with full repo access. The current immediate task is reframing the platform's value proposition from 'change management plus AI' to 'context-and-framework-aware AI integration' without a full redesign.

primary_user.name: Achim Imboden
primary_user.role: AI Adoption Architect, University of Basel
primary_user.communication_style: Direct, iterative, short messages. Concept-first then build. Frequent 'go' signals. Prefers Claude to self-critique before next step. English preferred.
primary_user.technical_profile: Vibe coder — strong product instincts, comfortable with HTML/CSS/JS, learning backend. Uses VS Code with Claude Code extension, also has Codex for cloud-based mechanical refactors.

project_overview.name: Change Management Platform
project_overview.purpose: An AI-augmented, browser-based learning platform for IMD's 5-week change management course. Walks practitioners through five connected units as one living change case (rather than five separate exercises).
project_overview.long_term_vision: Change Strategy Workbench — 'Notion for Change Management, but AI-augmented'
project_overview.strategic_positioning: Credible Completer — ship working tools, maintain low-frequency high-quality contact with IMD
project_overview.audience: IMD course participants, IMD learning team (potential institutional licensing), executive education context
project_overview.OLD_repo_DO_NOT_USE: blackmath88/change-management-platform (superseded — do not work on this one)

key_relationships.ala_sader: IMD learning coordinator, primary champion. Identified as key follow-up contact. Expressed genuine interest.
key_relationships.alessandra_marazzi: Internal IMD champion who introduced Achim to the learning team
key_relationships.sebastian: Collaborator, Laravel/PHP expert, joins in later phases

strategic_constraints.no_imd_branding: Zero IMD branding in the product. Tools complement (not replace) course workbooks.
strategic_constraints.no_commercial_solicitation: IMD channels are not for commercial outreach. Maintain restrained, no-pressure contact.
strategic_constraints.demo_scope_only: This is a demo platform. Backend will likely be replaced by customer's infrastructure. Don't over-invest in backend hardening.
strategic_constraints.frontend_durability: Frontend and content survive any backend swap — that's where investment compounds.

01_vision.purpose: Frame the change — case context, vision statement, north star

02_forces.purpose: Force field analysis — driving and restraining forces

03_stakeholders.purpose: Map who matters — power, interest, stance, readiness

05_nudges.purpose: Behavioural design — adoption map, EAST nudges, momentum signals

tech_stack.frontend: Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS (static, no build step, no framework)
tech_stack.design_system: cmt-system.css — Inter + Manrope fonts, navy/blue palette, warm neutral canvas, 240px sidebar

shared_files.cmt-client.js: Supabase client + module load/save + getCarryOver helper. Sends x-session-token header on every request.
shared_files.cmt-shell.js: Shared sidebar renderer (post-Batch 1 refactor). Mounted via
shared_files.cmt-system.css: Design tokens, shared styles, design system primitives

tech_stack.backend: Supabase (PostgreSQL + PostgREST). Two tables: projects, change_modules (JSONB data field).
tech_stack.hosting: GitHub Pages (auto-deploy on push to main, ~60s)
tech_stack.auth_model: Session-token-based. Random UUID in localStorage, sent as x-session-token HTTP header. Server-side RLS policies enforce per-token row access.
deferred_for_production: real authentication (Supabase Auth or customer SSO)

design_decisions_made.choice: Always land on Unit 1 (Vision) with a CHANGE CASE summary card at top + 'Continue to Unit XX →' button if project has progressed
design_decisions_made.reasoning: Methodology should frame the work without forcing it. Vision is the anchor of the case.

design_decisions_made.choice: 01-05 two-digit format, everywhere (sidebar, page-head eyebrow, breadcrumbs, stage labels)
design_decisions_made.reasoning: Five-week course, five units. Numbering makes the journey structure legible.

design_decisions_made.choice: Medium — sidebar split into OVERVIEW (Command Center) and WORKSPACE (units 01-05) with thin divider; dashboard page-head warmer + bigger title; no Unit XX eyebrow on dashboard
design_decisions_made.reasoning: Dashboard is a different mental mode (lobby) from working in a unit (room). Sidebar separator reflects this structural truth. Heavy redesign would solve a problem that doesn't exist.

design_decisions_made.choice: Binary dots — ● filled if unit has data, ○ outlined if empty. Active page gets separate highlight treatment.
design_decisions_made.reasoning: Honest signal that doesn't lie about completeness. Doesn't force linear progression. Real change work is iterative.

design_decisions_made.choice: Same binary dots on each tab inside a unit. AI tab gets NO dot (it's a tool, not a content area).
design_decisions_made.reasoning: Consistency with sidebar pattern. AI tab is structurally different — it uses populated sections, doesn't accumulate its own state.

design_decisions_made.choice: Option B — 2x2 grid of taller cards. Each card shows the actual task framing in plain text (~5-8 lines). Active card has highlighted border. Shared prompt textarea below shows the full prompt ready to copy.
design_decisions_made.reasoning: Original 4-cards-in-a-row design changed only an invisible TASK block at the bottom of a 600px prompt textarea. Users couldn't tell what changed when clicking modes. Now the variation is visible on the cards themselves before clicking.

design_decisions_made.choice: Pill-shaped nodes (9px colored dot + name) by default. Click to expand into 240px card. Click outside to collapse. Hover for tooltip. Fullscreen button + F shortcut + Esc to close.
design_decisions_made.reasoning: Original full-card design caused severe overlap with 7+ stakeholders in 'Manage closely' corner. Pills are 3x smaller. Only one card expanded at a time, so no concurrent overlap. Cluster is now readable at a glance for 12+ stakeholders.

design_decisions_made.choice: Larger (0.84rem), darker (0.55 alpha), with backdrop-blur background pill
design_decisions_made.reasoning: Previous treatment (0.66rem, 0.35 alpha) read as decoration, not structure. Labels need to anchor the chart visually.

design_decisions_made.choice: Per-unit accent colors used SPARINGLY — only on the unit number in sidebar (not the label) and on the page-head eyebrow. Active AI mode card gets subtle blue-violet tint. Command Center page-head gets soft warm tint. Otherwise platform stays calm and monochrome.
design_decisions_made.reasoning: Reviewer praised current restraint as professional. Add color as way-finding cues, not as theme.
Was visual noise. Faint blue grid lines on every page came from body background in cmt-system.css. Solid color base looks calmer.

imd_review_summary.overall_judgment: Strong enough to show IMD as a concept demonstrator. Frame it carefully — not a finished product, but a learning experience prototype.
imd_review_summary.central_strength: Participants build one living change case across the course, instead of completing disconnected workbook exercises.
imd_review_summary.biggest_weakness_named: Continuity. If later AI prompts show empty (not set) values, the 'connected case' story collapses.
imd_review_summary.key_reframe_recommended: From 'change management plus AI' to 'AI-ready change-management workspace'. The platform doesn't embed a chatbot — it generates structured, framework-aware prompts that work with whatever AI tool the user prefers (Claude, ChatGPT, institutional AI). This is a strategic strength: avoids procurement, hosting, data privacy, and model choice complexity.
imd_review_summary.exact_wording_endorsed: This is not an AI app. It is an AI-ready change-management workspace.
imd_review_summary.stronger_phrasing: The AI integration is technically lightweight but pedagogically specific.

tier_1_changes_status.status: Drafted as a Claude Code brief but Achim is unsure whether the brief was actually executed in his repo. Needs verification when Cowork has repo access.
items_in_tier_1: Empty-prompt states — replace (not set) blocks with contextual guidance pointing to upstream units
Homepage AI architecture explainer — one calm info section explaining the model-agnostic framing
AI tab tooltip — single italicized line at top of each unit's AI tab explaining 'use your own AI tool'
Light visual warmth — per-unit accent colors, AI card subtle tint, Command Center warm tint

current_immediate_task.user_request_verbatim: most importatn is now, really redesignin tth plattform from 'change managemt plus ai' to 'context and framework aware ai integration'. so they get, that point. its there now, but its not jumping in your eye. i think this quality is somewhat burried.
current_immediate_task.what_user_wants: The 'context-and-framework-aware AI integration' value proposition needs to surface as the platform's lead message. Currently it's present but visually buried — a reviewer doesn't see it in the first 2 seconds of looking at the page.
current_immediate_task.what_user_does_NOT_want: A full redesign. Targeted moves only. The platform's overall calm restraint should stay.
current_immediate_task.diagnostic_test_to_run_first: Open the live dashboard. Look at it for 3 seconds. Close the tab. Ask: 'what was that platform about?' If the answer doesn't include 'AI' or 'context-aware' or 'framework-aware' in the first sentence, the framing is buried.
current_immediate_task.why_cowork_now: This is a judgment-heavy, look-and-feel question. Diagnosing 'what's currently buried and why' requires seeing the actual rendered state, not reasoning from memory. Previous work was bounded execution; this is diagnosis.
current_immediate_task.approach: Start with diagnosis. Look at what's actually shipped on the dashboard and AI tabs. Identify where the eye lands first. Propose 2-3 specific surgical changes that would surface the AI framing. Implement those, not more.

phases_completed.summary: Initial build, enterprise redesign (Carbon/Anthropic aesthetic), Swiss policy demo seeding (Einheitskasse + 36-Hour-Week)
Demo visibility (Option B — demos always visible alongside personal projects), Supabase refactor (async init across all 5 units, CMT.loadAllModules)
Replaced overlapping cards with pills + click-to-expand + fullscreen modal. Solved the 'Manage closely corner is messy' problem.

Shell cleanup. Deleted unused cmt-shell.css and cmt-sidebar.js (legacy from v1 design). Extracted shared sidebar to cmt-shell.js, injected via #sidebar-mount. Removed dead href='#' links from dashboard. Added favicon.svg and basic Open Graph meta tags. Single commit, merged.

Shared infrastructure cleanup. Consolidated unit5 local exportProject into CMT.exportProject wrapper with pre-save flush. Added CMT.getCarryOver helper (additive, no retrofit). Standardized autosave debounce to 800ms across all units. Single commit, merged.

Pre-fix: RLS was on but all policies said qual=true (security theater — anyone with anon key could read/delete everything). Fix: added x-session-token header to every request from cmt-client.js. Updated 8 RLS policies on projects + change_modules tables to enforce per-token access via current_setting('request.headers')::json. Demo projects publicly readable but not writable through API. Verified live and working.

Numbering 01-05 everywhere. Sidebar OVERVIEW/WORKSPACE split. Vision summary card at top of Unit 1. Binary progress dots in sidebar AND tabs (no dot on AI tabs). AI tab redesign — 2x2 cards with visible task text. Per-unit accent colors. Stakeholder map quadrant labels strengthened. Background grid pattern removed.

Codex review found bug: cross-unit links don't preserve ?project= and unit pages never call setCurrentProjectId. Fix: each unit's init() now calls CMT.setCurrentProjectId(projectId) after resolving it. Also added README scope/limitations section documenting the demo-only auth model.
Empty-state UX + homepage AI architecture explainer + AI tab tooltips + light visual warmth. Brief was created. Achim is unsure whether it was actually run.

principles_and_patterns_to_preserve: Restraint is the house style. The platform's calm/serious tone is an asset that reviewers explicitly praise.
Don't redesign when targeted changes will work. Two examples: rejected 'heavy' Command Center differentiation in favor of medium; rejected stakeholder map redesign in favor of pill nodes.
Frontend is durable. Backend will be replaced. Don't over-invest in backend hardening for production scenarios that may never happen on this stack.
Idempotent seed SQL (DELETE-then-INSERT with fixed UUIDs) is the demo data pattern.
Demos are realistic, deeply-detailed Swiss policy cases with named real-world stakeholders. They're not made up.
Always ship working tools. Maintain credibility through delivery, not concept-pitching.
Plan-before-execute when working with Claude Code. Get the plan approved, then implement.
Surface ambiguity, don't paper over it. The user prefers 'I noticed X, here are 2 options' over 'I went ahead and did Y'.
Use claude.ai memory + JSON handoffs for cross-session continuity. The handoff structure works.
Code Review pattern: Codex flags, Claude (in chat or Code) writes targeted Claude Code prompts to fix. Two-step pattern works well.
things_NOT_to_do: Don't redesign the whole platform — targeted moves only
Don't migrate to React/Next.js — explicitly deferred
Don't add a build step or framework
Don't keep extending per-unit accent colors beyond the 5 spots already in use (sidebar number + page-head eyebrow)
Don't add login, dashboards, embedded AI APIs, team collaboration, grading, or admin features before showing IMD
Don't paint the whole app with color — restraint is the asset
Don't use IMD channels for commercial outreach
Don't position this as 'finished software' or 'commercial SaaS'
Don't claim AI replaces faculty or workbooks
Don't spend time on backend hardening that may be thrown away when customer takes ownership of hosting

claims_to_use: Support for reflection, coaching, group work, and post-course continuation

open_questions_to_revisit: Demo content audit: are both Swiss demos truly fully populated through Unit 4? (36-Hour case is intentionally Unit-5-empty as the in-progress case)
Does the 'AI architecture' framing currently land in 3 seconds on the live dashboard, or is it buried as the reviewer suggested?
Backend conversation with customer — what stack? Identity? Privacy requirements? This conversation reshapes everything downstream.
Stakeholder map: known small bug — expanded card overflows canvas at edges (deferred, not urgent)
Keyboard accessibility on stakeholder UI (Codex flagged P2, deferred until post-customer conversation)
first_message_to_paste_into_cowork:
Hi. I've been working on this Change Management Platform with another Claude. The repo is cloned in this Cowork session. I'm attaching a handoff JSON with full context.

My immediate task: the platform's strongest insight — 'context-and-framework-aware AI integration, not an AI app' — is technically present but visually buried. A reviewer flagged this. I want to surface it as the lead message without redesigning the platform.

Please start with diagnosis, not action:

1. Read the handoff JSON for full context.
2. Look at the actual current state of index.html (the dashboard) and the AI tab in any unit (try unit3-stakeholders.html).
3. Tell me what your eye lands on first when scanning the dashboard for 2-3 seconds. Where does the AI framing actually live now? Is it visible as the lead, or buried below the fold / in muted text / mixed with everything else?
4. Propose 2-3 specific surgical changes that would surface the AI framing — not a redesign. The platform's overall calm restraint should stay.

Before implementing anything, show me the diagnosis and the proposal. I'll steer from there.
